walkthrough.title: Meet the Tiles
walkthrough.text: Welcome to A Gentle Rain. This is one of the most peaceful games you'll ever play. There's no competition, no stress — just the quiet satisfaction of watching flowers bloom on lily pads. Let's get set up. First, find the stack of 28 lake tiles. They have two sides. The front side shows a beautiful lily pad with four colored half-flowers along the edges. The back side is a calm green with subtle raindrop ripples. Go ahead and pick one up and look at both sides.
walkthrough.image_caption: Lake tiles — front side with lily pad flowers and green raindrop back

walkthrough.title: Shuffle the Tiles
walkthrough.text: Now shuffle all 28 tiles face-down, green side up, so you cannot see the flower patterns. You can spread them out on the table and mix them around, then gather them into a neat stack. This is your draw pile for the whole game.

walkthrough.title: Set Out the Blossom Tokens
walkthrough.text: Find the 8 wooden blossom tokens. These are beautiful little discs, each showing a different colorful flower. Lay them all out face-up near the draw pile where everyone can see and reach them. Each one is unique — take a moment to admire them. These are the blossoms you are trying to coax into blooming during the game.

walkthrough.title: Place Your First Tile
walkthrough.text: Draw one tile from the top of the stack and flip it face-up. Place it in the center of the table. This is your starting tile and the beginning of your lake. Every tile you place from now on will connect to this one, growing outward like ripples on water.

walkthrough.title: Ready to Play
walkthrough.text: That is the entire setup. If you are playing solo, you will draw and place tiles one at a time. If you are playing with a friend, decide who goes first — you will take turns drawing and placing tiles together, cooperatively. The goal is simple and beautiful: place tiles so that matching colors meet at intersections, causing flowers to bloom. Let's learn how.

summary.title: Meet the Tiles
bullets: 28 lake tiles — front shows lily pad with 4 colored half-flower edges
Back is green with raindrop ripples

summary.title: Shuffle the Tiles
bullets: Shuffle all 28 tiles face-down (green side up)
Stack them into a draw pile

summary.title: Set Out the Blossom Tokens
bullets: Place all 8 wooden blossom tokens face-up nearby
Each token shows a unique flower

summary.title: Place Your First Tile
bullets: Draw 1 tile, flip face-up, place in center of table
This is the starting tile for your lake

summary.title: Ready to Play
bullets: Solo: draw and place tiles one at a time
2-player: alternate turns, playing cooperatively
Goal: match colors at intersections to bloom flowers

walkthrough.title: Draw a Tile
walkthrough.text: On your turn, draw one tile from the top of the face-down stack. Flip it over so you can see the lily pad side with its four colored half-flower edges. Take a moment to look at the colors and think about where it could go.

walkthrough.title: Place the Tile
walkthrough.text: Now place your tile next to any tile already on the table. It must touch at least one existing tile along an edge. Here is the key rule: wherever two tiles touch, the colored half-flowers on their touching edges must match. You can rotate your tile freely before placing it — turn it around to find the best fit. Tiles do not need to match on every side, only on the sides that actually touch another tile.
walkthrough.image_caption: Tiles showing colored half-flower edges that must match when adjacent

walkthrough.title: Mandatory Placement
walkthrough.text: If you can legally place your tile anywhere in the lake, you must place it. You cannot choose to discard a tile that has a valid spot. Take your time and look carefully — there may be a placement you did not notice at first.

walkthrough.title: Discarding a Tile
walkthrough.text: Sometimes you will draw a tile that simply cannot fit anywhere legally. Every touching edge must match colors, and if there is no spot where that works, you must discard the tile to a face-up discard pile. This is not a failure — it is just the gentle rhythm of the rain. Draw again on your next turn.

walkthrough.title: Blooming a Flower
walkthrough.text: This is the magical moment. When you complete a two-by-two square of four tiles, look at the center intersection where all four tile corners meet. Each corner has a quarter-circle cutout, and together they form a small circular space. If the four half-flowers surrounding that intersection create a complete color match, a blossom blooms. Choose the matching blossom token from the supply and place it in that intersection. It is a lovely little moment every time it happens.
walkthrough.image_caption: Blossom tokens placed when four tile corners complete a color match

walkthrough.title: One Bloom per Intersection
walkthrough.text: Each intersection can only hold one blossom token. If the matching blossom token has already been placed at a different intersection earlier in the game, then no bloom happens at this new intersection. That is okay — there are 8 unique tokens and many intersections to create.

walkthrough.title: Game End
walkthrough.text: The game ends in one of two ways. Either you place all 8 blossom tokens — congratulations, that is a perfect game and a beautiful achievement — or you run out of tiles in the draw pile. When the last tile is drawn and placed or discarded, the game is over.

walkthrough.title: Scoring
walkthrough.text: Your final score is the number of blossom tokens you placed plus the number of tiles remaining in the draw pile. In a perfect game where all 8 blossoms bloom, you also score points for every tile you did not need. The highest possible score comes from blooming all 8 flowers as quickly as possible, leaving the most tiles in the stack. But honestly, just blooming flowers is its own reward.

summary.title: Draw a Tile
bullets: Draw 1 tile from the face-down stack
Flip it face-up to see the lily pad side

summary.title: Place the Tile
bullets: Place adjacent to any existing tile (at least 1 edge touching)
All touching edges must match colors
Rotate freely before placing

summary.title: Mandatory Placement
bullets: If a legal placement exists, you must place the tile
You cannot voluntarily discard a playable tile

summary.title: Discarding a Tile
bullets: If no legal placement exists, discard the tile face-up
Discarded tiles are out of the game

summary.title: Blooming a Flower
bullets: Complete a 2×2 square of tiles
If colors match at the center intersection, a blossom blooms
Place the matching wooden blossom token in the intersection

summary.title: One Bloom per Intersection
bullets: Each intersection holds at most 1 blossom token
If the matching token is already placed elsewhere, no bloom occurs

summary.title: Game End
bullets: All 8 blossom tokens placed = perfect game
OR all 28 tiles drawn = game over

summary.title: Scoring
bullets: Score = blossom tokens placed + tiles remaining in stack
Perfect game: 8 blossoms + leftover tiles
Higher score = bloomed faster with more tiles to spare

walkthrough.title: Think About Rotation
walkthrough.text: The single most important decision each turn is how to rotate your tile before placing it. Each tile has four different colored edges, and rotating it changes which colors face which direction. Before you place, look at the open edges around your lake and find the spot where your tile creates the most color matches — especially matches that are building toward a two-by-two square.

walkthrough.title: Build in a Grid, Not a Line
walkthrough.text: New players often place tiles in a long line or L-shape. This limits your blooming opportunities because blossoms only appear in two-by-two squares. Try to build outward in a compact grid shape from the start. The more two-by-two squares you create, the more chances you have for blossoms to bloom.

walkthrough.title: Watch the Blossom Tokens
walkthrough.text: Keep an eye on which blossom tokens are still available. If a particular flower color has already been placed, there is no benefit to completing another intersection of that same color. Focus your placement on creating intersections that match the colors of blossom tokens still in the supply.

walkthrough.title: Set Up Future Blooms
walkthrough.text: Sometimes the best play is not to bloom right now, but to set up multiple blooming opportunities for future turns. If you can place a tile that creates two or three partially-completed intersections, you give yourself better odds with upcoming tile draws. Think one or two tiles ahead when you can.

walkthrough.title: Cooperating with a Partner
walkthrough.text: In two-player mode, communication is your best tool. Talk about which colors you are trying to build toward and where you see promising intersections forming. Since you cannot control what tile you draw, coordinating your placements helps you make the most of whatever the stack gives you. There is no hidden information — plan together openly.

summary.title: Think About Rotation
bullets: Rotation is your main decision each turn
Look at open edges and find the best color match
Prioritize placements that build toward 2×2 squares

summary.title: Build in a Grid, Not a Line
bullets: Compact grid shape = more blooming opportunities
Lines and L-shapes limit intersections
Build outward evenly from the center

summary.title: Watch the Blossom Tokens
bullets: Track which tokens are still available
No benefit completing a color whose token is already placed
Focus on colors with unused tokens

summary.title: Set Up Future Blooms
bullets: Sometimes setup is better than an immediate bloom
Create multiple partially-complete intersections
Think 1-2 tiles ahead

summary.title: Cooperating with a Partner
bullets: Talk about which colors you are building toward
No hidden information — plan together openly
Coordinate placements to maximize bloom chances

entries.definition: One of 28 tiles in the game. Each tile is nearly square with quarter-circle cutouts at the corners. The front shows a lily pad with four colored half-flower edges. The back is green with subtle raindrop ripple patterns. Tiles are drawn one at a time from a face-down stack.
The face-down side of each lake tile, showing a calm green color with circular raindrop ripple patterns. Tiles are shuffled and stacked green-side-up to form the draw pile.

One of 8 unique wooden flower discs. Each shows a different colorful flower design. Blossom tokens are placed at tile intersections when a color match is completed. Collecting all 8 is a perfect game.

Each tile has four edges, each displaying a colored half-flower. When two tiles are placed adjacent, their touching edges must show the same color. The colors correspond to different flower types visible on the lily pad artwork.

A tile must be placed so that at least one edge touches an existing tile. All touching edges must match colors. The tile may be rotated freely before being placed. If a legal placement exists, it is mandatory.

Before placing a tile, you may rotate it to any orientation. Since each edge has a different color, rotation changes which colors face each direction. This is the primary strategic decision each turn.

When a drawn tile cannot be legally placed anywhere in the lake (no spot where all touching edges match), it must be discarded face-up. Discarded tiles are out of the game and count as played for scoring purposes.

When a 2×2 square of four tiles is completed and the colors at the center intersection match, a blossom blooms. The matching wooden blossom token is placed at that intersection. This is the core scoring mechanism of the game.

The point where four tile corners meet in a 2×2 square. Each tile has quarter-circle cutouts at its corners, creating a small circular space at the intersection. If the surrounding half-flowers match in color, a blossom token is placed here.

Achieved when all 8 blossom tokens are placed before the tile stack runs out. A perfect game means every unique flower has bloomed somewhere in your lake. The score for a perfect game is 8 plus the number of tiles remaining in the stack.
Calculated as: number of blossom tokens placed + number of unplayed tiles remaining in the draw stack. A higher score means you bloomed more flowers and did so efficiently, with tiles to spare.
The default way to play A Gentle Rain. One player draws and places all tiles, trying to bloom as many flowers as possible. The meditative pace makes it a perfect quiet activity.

Two players take turns drawing and placing tiles from the shared stack. Both players work together toward the same goal of blooming flowers. Communication and coordination are encouraged. The score is shared.
The face-down stack of shuffled lake tiles. Players draw one tile at a time from the top. The game ends when the draw pile is exhausted or all 8 blossoms bloom.
